title: T-063 — document Radix colon-id axe-core quirk in packages/ui/TESTING.md (FT-003)
prompt:
# T-063 — document Radix colon-id axe-core quirk in packages/ui/TESTING.md (FT-003)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.y
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-003** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P2** (XS effort)

## Goal
Add a clearly-titled section to packages/ui/TESTING.md documenting the Radix colon-id axe quirk and the useSanitiseRadixIds remedy.

## Implementation contract
1. Edit `packages/ui/TESTING.md` (create the file if absent) with a section titled `## Radix colon-id axe-core quirk`.
2. Include: symptom (axe assertion failure message), root cause (one-line), workaround (import useSanitiseRadixIds), and worked example with vitest snippet.
3. Update the unit test for the hook to include a doc-block linking to the new section.

## Files (scope)
- packages/ui/TESTING.md [create-or-modify]
- packages/ui/src/__tests__/use-sanitise-radix-ids.test.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a reader opens packages/ui/TESTING.md, when they search for `colon`, then they find a section explaining the Radix quirk with cause + workaround + code snippet.
2. Given the existing useSanitiseRadixIds test, when it runs, then a top-of-file JSDoc block links to the TESTING.md section.
3. Given `pnpm --filter @monopilot/ui test` is run, then all existing tests still pass.

## Test strategy
- RED: no new test logic; add the doc-block link and a vitest `describe.skip` placeholder that becomes a real assertion if doc-block is removed.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/ui test` to confirm no regression.

## Risk red lines
- Do not change the hook implementation — docs-only task.
- Do not modify ESLint or axe-core configuration.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Document the Radix colon-id quirk that breaks axe-core selectors and how the existing useSanitiseRadixIds hook works around it.
pipeline_inputs.details: Radix primitives emit IDs containing a literal colon (e.g. `radix-:r1:`) which breaks axe-core's CSS selector path. T-031 shipped a `useSanitiseRadixIds` workaround but it is undocumented. Add a `packages/ui/TESTING.md` section explaining the bug, why we sanitise, where to import the hook, and a worked example.
out_of_scope: No new tests beyond doc-block linkage.
acceptance_criteria: Given a reader opens packages/ui/TESTING.md, when they search for `colon`, then they find a section explaining the Radix quirk with cause + workaround + code snippet.
Given the existing useSanitiseRadixIds test, when it runs, then a top-of-file JSDoc block links to the TESTING.md section.
Given `pnpm --filter @monopilot/ui test` is run, then all existing tests still pass.

Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/ui test` to confirm no regression.
